top_signals.title: US Ends Hormuz Blockade, Downplays Tolls as 60-Day Clock Starts
top_signals.summary: The US-Iran interim peace deal took effect and shipping started returning to the Strait of Hormuz as the US declared an end to its blockade and a complex negotiating period over Tehran’s nuclear program began in earnest. Mona Yacoubian, Director and senior adviser of the Middle East Program at the Center for Strategic and International Studies joins Balance of Power to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Fed Officials Edge Closer to Rate Hikes As Warsh Takes The Reigns
top_signals.summary: Former Federal Reserve Vice Chair Lael Brainard joined Balance of Power to discuss  Kevin Warsh's first meeting as Fed Chair. She said several of the task forces Fed Chair Kevin Warsh announced yesterday could be ways he tries to move the committee more into a direction of lowering rates. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: CoinDesk 20 performance update: Stellar (XLM) jumps 10% while index declines
Energy Fuels lands $725M Pentagon loan for rare earths boost
top_signals.summary: Energy Fuels shares jumped after securing financing from the US government to expand local rare earth processing capacity.

top_signals.title: CORE POWER to Assess Reactor Tech for Floating Nuclear Power Plants
top_signals.summary: CORE POWER has launched a feasibility study to evaluate the use of BWX Technologies' mPower small modular reactor (SMR) technology in floating nuclear power plants designed to be built in shipyards and deployed near energy demand centers.The study will assess technical…
